title: Permutation tests for statistical significance
text: steps “Introduction transclust” vignette. transclust package comes sample dataset can used demonstration purposes. dataset contains epidemiological information set isolates. load dataset, can use following command: first read sequence file prepared using procedure described Hawken et al. (2022). can read sequence file using ape’s read.dna function: Now sequence file loaded, can extract variable positions alignment. dna_var_pos variable contains logical vector indicating columns alignment variable. dna_pt_labels variable contains labels sequences alignment, trace_mat matrix containing trace data isolates. valid set labels determined checking labels dna_pt_labels variable present trace_mat matrix. can subset alignment include variable positions isolates valid labels: use helper function get_snp_dist_matrix() calculate SNP distance matrix isolates: snp_dist variable contains SNP distance matrix, square matrix entry represents number SNP differences two isolates. diagonal entries zero, represent distance isolate .
code: load(system.file("extdata", "example.Rdata", package = "transclust")) dna_aln <- read.dna(system.file("extdata", "example.fasta", package = "transclust"), format = "fasta") # Get all variable positions in the alignment var_pos <- apply(dna_aln, 2, function(x) sum(x == x[1]) < nrow(dna_aln)) # Only keep those labels that are in the trace matrix valid_labels <- dna_pt_labels[labels(dna_aln)] %in% colnames(trace_mat) dna_var <- dna_aln[valid_labels, var_pos] snp_dist <- get_snp_dist_matrix(dna_var)

title: Permutation tests for statistical significance
text: transclust offers two methods determine transmission clusters: include hard SNP-threshold based approach threshold-free shared-variant based algorithm described Hawken et al. (2022). vignette, focus latter method statistical analysis. first create phylogenetic tree using SNP distance matrix use along epidemiological data get transmission clusters: gives us clusters can now use statistical analysis.
code: phylo_tree_pars <- get_phylo_tree(dna_var, snp_dist, "pars") #> Final p-score 2122 after  8 nni operations clusters <- get_tn_clusters_sv_index(     dna_var, snp_dist, ip_seqs_3days, ip_seqs,     dna_pt_labels, dates, phylo_tree_pars )

title: Permutation tests for statistical significance
text: transclust package includes function performs permutation tests assess statistical significance clustering results. list several important prooperties clusters computed observed data permuted data. permutation test performed randomly permuting labels isolates recalculating clustering results. process repeated specified number times generate distribution clustering results null hypothesis. observed clustering results compared distribution determine whether significantly different expected chance. tests informative, clusters permuted certain constraints. can divide patients two “classes”: index convert. Index patients first cluster, convert patients infected index patient. permutation tests performed randomly permuting labels isolates changing proportions index convert patients clusters. done ensure permutation tests meaningful take account structure data. permutation tests computationally intensive can take time, better performance can run parallel multiple cores *nix systems (Windows support parallel execution manner). However, since vignette platforms, run test single core. cluster_property_perm_test() function takes following arguments: perm_props object contains results permutation tests, including observed permuted values property. cluster_property_perm_test() relies cluster_properties() function calculate properties clusters along additional properties calculated permutation tests. know properties? can see properties looking perm_props object: Lets choose look one , "Median Max_genetic_distance". just median maximum genetic distance isolates cluster. can plot observed value permuted values see compares:  can see observed value significantly lower permuted values, indicating observed clusters mostly closely related isolates. p-value calculated proportion permuted values less equal observed value. low p-value indicates observed value significantly different expected chance. p-value see 0.01, lower commonly used significance level 0.05. mean case? Well, maximum genetic distance isolates cluster significantly lower expected chance. suggests clusters made closely related isolates, consistent idea part transmission chain. important finding, suggests clusters just random groups isolates, rather represent real transmission events. Let’s look another example, time statistically significant. can look "Median Number_of_converts_after_index" property, median number converts index case cluster. property expected significantly different expected chance, directly related clustering results fact property underlying data. can plot observed value permuted values see compares:  can see p-value close 1, indicating observed value significantly different expected chance. permutations designed keep similar proportions index convert patients clusters, expect property significantly different expected chance. Thus permutation tests provide useful way assess statistical significance clustering results, can help identify properties significantly different expected chance, helping us understand signatures transmission data.
code: nperm <- 500 perm_props <- cluster_property_perm_test(     clusters, trace_mat, dna_pt_labels, ip_seqs, ip_seqs_3days, dates, snp_dist,     nperm = nperm, floor_trace = floor_mat, room_trace = room_mat, num_cores = 1 ) colnames(perm_props) #>  [1] "Median Number_of_patients"                                 #>  [2] "Median Number_of_start_indexes"                            #>  [3] "Median Number_of_non-start_indexes"                        #>  [4] "Median Number_of_study_start_indexes"                      #>  [5] "Median Number_of_converts"                                 #>  [6] "Median Number_of_initial_converts"                         #>  [7] "Median Number_of_later_converts"                           #>  [8] "Median Cluster_duration"                                   #>  [9] "Median Mean_genetic_distance"                              #> [10] "Median Median_genetic_distance"                            #> [11] "Median Max_genetic_distance"                               #> [12] "Median Number_of_converts_after_index"                     #> [13] "Median Number_of_initial_converts_after_index"             #> [14] "Median Number_of_converts_after_index_seq"                 #> [15] "Median Number_of_initial_converts_after_index_seq"         #> [16] "Median Number_of_converts_with_source"                     #> [17] "Median Number_of_initial_converts_with_source"             #> [18] "Median Number_of_converts_with_floor_source"               #> [19] "Median Number_of_initial_converts_with_floor_source"       #> [20] "Median Number_of_converts_with_room_source"                #> [21] "Median Number_of_initial_converts_with_room_source"        #> [22] "Median Time_to_first_acquisition"                          #> [23] "Median Time_to_last_acquisition"                           #> [24] "Median Median_time_to_acquisition"                         #> [25] "Median Time_from_index_to_first_convert"                   #> [26] "Median Time_from_index_to_last_convert"                    #> [27] "Per convert Number_of_converts_with_source"                #> [28] "Per convert Number_of_converts_with_floor_source"          #> [29] "Per convert Number_of_converts_with_room_source"           #> [30] "Per convert Number_of_converts_after_index"                #> [31] "Per convert Number_of_initial_converts_with_source"        #> [32] "Per convert Number_of_initial_converts_with_floor_source"  #> [33] "Per convert Number_of_initial_converts_with_room_source"   #> [34] "Per convert Number_of_initial_converts_after_index"        #> [35] "Fraction of clusters Number_of_converts_with_source"       #> [36] "Fraction of clusters Number_of_converts_with_floor_source" #> [37] "Fraction of clusters Number_of_converts_with_room_source"  #> [38] "Fraction of clusters Number_of_converts_after_index" prop_name <- colnames(perm_props)[11]  perm_vals <- perm_props[1:nperm, prop_name] observed_val <- perm_props[nperm + 1, prop_name]  upper_p <- (sum(perm_vals <= observed_val) + 1) / (nperm + 1) lower_p <- (sum(perm_vals >= observed_val) + 1) / (nperm + 1) prop_pval <- min(upper_p, lower_p)  ggplot(data = data.frame(x = perm_vals), aes(x = x)) +     geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count)), bins = 10, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +     geom_point(aes(x = observed_val, y = 0), shape = 23, size = 5, fill = "red") +     annotate("text", x = observed_val, y = 0.5, label = "Observed", vjust = -1) +     labs(         title = paste0("Permutation tests for ", prop_name, "\n p-value: ", round(prop_pval, 3)),         x = prop_name,         y = "Count"     ) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5)) prop_name <- colnames(perm_props)[14]  perm_vals <- perm_props[1:nperm, prop_name] observed_val <- perm_props[nperm + 1, prop_name]  upper_p <- (sum(perm_vals <= observed_val) + 1) / (nperm + 1) lower_p <- (sum(perm_vals >= observed_val) + 1) / (nperm + 1) prop_pval <- min(upper_p, lower_p)  ggplot(data = data.frame(x = perm_vals), aes(x = x)) +     geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(count)), bins = 10, fill = "lightblue", color = "black") +     geom_point(aes(x = observed_val, y = 0), shape = 23, size = 5, fill = "red") +     annotate("text", x = observed_val, y = 0.5, label = "Observed", vjust = -1) +     labs(         title = paste0("Permutation tests for ", prop_name, "\n p-value: ", round(prop_pval, 3)),         x = prop_name,         y = "Count"     ) +     theme_minimal() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Introduction to transclust
text: transclust package comes sample dataset can used demonstration purposes. dataset contains epidemiological information set isolates. load dataset, can use following command: first read sequence file prepared using procedure described Hawken et al. (2022). can read sequence file using ape’s read.dna function: Now sequence file loaded, can extract variable positions alignment. dna_var_pos variable contains logical vector indicating columns alignment variable. dna_pt_labels variable contains labels sequences alignment, trace_mat matrix containing trace data isolates. valid set labels determined checking labels dna_pt_labels variable present trace_mat matrix. can subset alignment include variable positions isolates valid labels: use helper function get_snp_dist_matrix() calculate SNP distance matrix isolates: snp_dist variable contains SNP distance matrix, square matrix entry represents number SNP differences two isolates. diagonal entries zero, represent distance isolate .
code: load(system.file("extdata", "example.Rdata", package = "transclust")) dna_aln <- read.dna(system.file("extdata", "example.fasta", package = "transclust"), format = "fasta") # Get all variable positions in the alignment var_pos <- apply(dna_aln, 2, function(x) sum(x == x[1]) < nrow(dna_aln)) # Only keep those labels that are in the trace matrix valid_labels <- dna_pt_labels[labels(dna_aln)] %in% colnames(trace_mat) dna_var <- dna_aln[valid_labels, var_pos] snp_dist <- get_snp_dist_matrix(dna_var)

what: Clustering isolates using a hard SNP threshold
title: Introduction to transclust
text: Now ’ve loaded data, can start clustering isolates. First, want use hard SNP threshold cluster isolates. get_tn_clusters_snp_thresh() function takes SNP distance matrix threshold value input returns list clusters. use threshold 10 SNPs example: can visualize clusters phylogenetic tree using plot_clusters_phylo() function. , first need generate phylogenetic tree. can using get_phylo_tree() function. use maximum parsimony method example:  isn’t SNP threshold approach. method tries preserve clusters within phylogenetic tree structure, can also cluster isolates just based SNP distance matrix regard
code: clusters_snp <- get_tn_clusters_snp_thresh(snp_dist, 10) # Generate a parsimony tree phylo_tree <- get_phylo_tree(dna_var, snp_dist, "pars") plot_clusters_phylo(phylo_tree, clusters_snp)

what: Clustering isolates using a threshold-free approach
title: Introduction to transclust
text: addition hard SNP threshold approach, can also use threshold-free approach cluster isolates. package implements method described Hawken et al. (2022) get_tn_clusters_sv_index() function. method uses threshold-free approach identify clusters based structure phylogenetic tree. use parsimony tree example. function takes inputs SNP threshold method: clusters_sv object contains clusters identified using threshold-free approach. plot shows phylogenetic tree clusters highlighted.
code: clusters_sv <- get_tn_clusters_sv_index(     dna_var, snp_dist, ip_seqs_3days, ip_seqs,     dna_pt_labels, dates, phylo_tree ) plot_clusters_phylo(phylo_tree, clusters_sv)

title: Introduction to transclust
text: two trees look quite different. can compare clusters generated using two methods using compare_clusters() function. function generates heatmap showing overlap clusters indicating proportion isolates cluster also present clusters generated method. can use function, need remove singleton clusters, clusters contain one isolate – useful comparison:
code: # remove singleton clusters from both clustering methods clusters_snp <- remove_singleton_clusters(clusters_snp) clusters_sv <- remove_singleton_clusters(clusters_sv) # compare the clusters compare_clusters(clusters_snp, clusters_sv) +     # change the color palette     scale_fill_paletteer_c("grDevices::Mint", direction = -1) +     # add a title     ggtitle("Comparison of clusters using SNP threshold and threshold-free methods") +     # add x and y axis labels     xlab("Threshold-free clusters") +     ylab("SNP threshold clusters") +     # center the title and rotate x axis labels     theme(         plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5),         axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)     ) #> → heatmap built with `geom_tile()`

what: Some statistical analysis of the clustering results
title: Introduction to transclust
text: transclust package also provides functions statistical analysis clustering results. example, can use intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis() function calculate genetic variation within cluster: dataframe columns total number variable sites, also proportions six types base substitutions present cluster. isn’t intuitive just look , can plot . First, drop possible NA values. plot, want compare relative frequencies different types base substitutions, also exclude first column - total number variable sites. subset data frame include first rows clarity plot: can plot proportions type base substitution cluster. , ggplot2 package used create stacked bar plot:  can clearly see specific mutational signatures emerge clusters compared population whole.
code: mut_var_df <- intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis(clusters_sv, dna_aln, var_pos) mut_var_df <- head(na.omit(mut_var_df)[, -1]) # Add column for cluster using rownames before pivoting mut_var_df$cluster <- row.names(mut_var_df)  # Convert the data frame to long format for ggplot mut_var_df_long <- pivot_longer(     mut_var_df,     cols = -cluster,     names_to = "mutation_type",     values_to = "proportion" )  # Create the stacked bar plot ggplot(mut_var_df_long, aes(x = cluster, y = proportion, fill = mutation_type)) +     geom_bar(stat = "identity") +     labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Proportion", fill = "Mutation Type") +     scale_fill_paletteer_d("ggsci::lanonc_lancet")

what: An R package for transmission cluster analysis for pathogens
title: An R package for transmission cluster analysis for pathogens
text: project involve implementation evaluation algorithms detecting transmission clusters healthcare settings using genomic data. [!WARNING] project still early stages development API may change without notice.

title: An R package for transmission cluster analysis for pathogens
text: transclust available CRAN yet, can install GitHub using devtools:
code: devtools::install_github("theabhirath/transclust")

title: An R package for transmission cluster analysis for pathogens
text: also provide epidemiological trace data isolates dataset. used conjunction genomic data identify clusters transmission. data provided Rdata file, can loaded R using load() function. multiple data objects, including: trace_mat: matrix trace data. Rows days, columns patients. floor_mat: matrix tracking patient locations floor hospital. Rows days, columns patients. room_mat: matrix tracking room assignments patients. Rows days, columns patients. dates: vector admission dates patient. dna_pt_labels: vector mapping sequence IDs isolates patient IDs. ip_seqs: vector sequence IDs correspond intake patient sequences presumed imported. ip_seqs_3days: vector sequence IDs correspond intake patient sequences tested positive within 3 days admission.

what: Example dataset for using the tool
title: An R package for transmission cluster analysis for pathogens
text: example dataset sequence alignment file smaller subset data generated ST258 isolates. provided inst/extdata directory package example.FASTA. epidemiological data matching isolates provided example dataset example.Rdata. Dates shifted relative start date protect patient privacy, structure data complete dataset. example dataset used package documentation vignettes demonstrate use package. example dataset small, small enough still manageable testing demonstration purposes. subset complete dataset, much larger contains sequence types. example dataset used demonstrate functionality package without overwhelming users much data. Locally, tested M1 MacBook Air, example dataset takes minute run clustering algorithm generate phylogenetic tree, reasonable amount time demonstration.

title: Produces summary plots regarding genetic distances within and between transmission clusters. — cluster_genetic_context
text: clusters vector named sequence IDs values indicating cluster (subtree) sequence belongs . seq2pt named vector mapping sequence IDs patient IDs. ip_seqs vector sequence IDs corresponding intake positive patients. snp_dist matrix SNP distances isolates. prefix descriptor indicating clusters generated (used name figure outputs).

title: Produces summary plots regarding genetic distances within and between transmission clusters. — cluster_genetic_context
text: matrix containing intra-cluster inter-cluster genetic distances.

title: Compute various cluster-level properties — cluster_properties
text: Calculates various epidemiological genetic characteristics given set sequence IDs single cluster. includes counts patients, convert vs. index statuses, genetic distances, cluster duration, timing-based metrics time first/last acquisition.

title: Compute various cluster-level properties — cluster_properties
code: cluster_properties(   cluster_seqs,   pt_trace,   seq2pt,   ip_pt_seqs,   ip_seqs,   snp_dist,   dates,   floor_trace = NULL,   room_trace = NULL )

title: Compute various cluster-level properties — cluster_properties
text: cluster_seqs character vector sequence IDs belonging cluster. pt_trace data frame matrix patient-level trace data (rows days, columns patient IDs). seq2pt named character vector mapping sequence IDs patient IDs (names must sequence IDs, values must patient IDs). ip_pt_seqs character vector sequence IDs corresponding patients tested positive admission (\"intake positive\"). ip_seqs character vector sequence IDs presumed imported outside (subset ip_pt_seqs similar). snp_dist matrix SNP distances isolates (row column names match sequence IDs). dates named numeric vector isolate dates sequence ID. floor_trace (Optional) data frame matrix floor-level tracing. room_trace (Optional) data frame matrix room-level tracing.

title: Compute various cluster-level properties — cluster_properties
text: named numeric vector containing various cluster-level properties. list properties computed given details section.

title: Compute various cluster-level properties — cluster_properties
text: function computes following properties given cluster: Number_of_patients Number_of_start_indexes Number_of_non-start_indexes Number_of_study_start_indexes Number_of_converts Number_of_initial_converts Number_of_later_converts Cluster_duration Mean_genetic_distance Median_genetic_distance Max_genetic_distance Number_of_converts_after_index Number_of_initial_converts_after_index Number_of_converts_after_index_seq Number_of_initial_converts_after_index_seq Number_of_converts_with_source Number_of_initial_converts_with_source Number_of_converts_with_floor_source Number_of_initial_converts_with_floor_source Number_of_converts_with_room_source Number_of_initial_converts_with_room_source Time_to_first_acquisition Time_to_last_acquisition Median_time_to_acquisition Time_from_index_to_first_convert Time_from_index_to_last_convert can used analysis visualization, including example permutation tests assess significance observed cluster properties.

code: cluster_property_perm_test(   clusters,   pt_trace,   seq2pt,   ip_pt_seqs,   ip_seqs,   dates,   snp_dist,   nperm = 1000,   floor_trace = NULL,   room_trace = NULL,   num_cores = detectCores() - 1 )

title: Permutation Test for Cluster Properties — cluster_property_perm_test
text: clusters named numeric vector names sequence IDs values subtrees defining cluster. pt_trace matrix data frame rows representing days columns representing patients. seq2pt named vector mapping sequence IDs patient IDs. ip_pt_seqs vector sequence IDs corresponding intake positive patients. ip_seqs vector sequence IDs corresponding intake patient sequences presumed imported. dates vector isolate dates named sequence IDs. snp_dist matrix SNP distances isolates. nperm number permutations perform (default 1000). floor_trace optional floor trace (rows: days, columns: patients). room_trace optional room trace (rows: days, columns: patients). num_cores number cores use parallel processing (default one less total number cores). *nix systems work Windows - Windows, set num_cores = 1.

title: Permutation Test for Cluster Properties — cluster_property_perm_test
text: matrix permutation statistics. Rows correspond permutation (last row containing observed statistic) columns correspond computed properties.

text: Clustering performed identify maximal clusters containing single intake-positive patient occurs cluster converts. clustering metric number shared variants, clusters can multiple intake-positive patients share identical number variants cluster members intake-positive patients occur converts. clustering also requires clusters defined least one shared variant isolates .

text: dna_aln DNA alignment object class DNAbin. snp_dist matrix SNP distances isolates constructed using model DNA evolution. See get_snp_dist_matrix useful function generate . ip_seqs vector sequence IDs correspond intake patient sequences presumed imported. ip_pt_seqs vector sequence IDs correspond intake-positive patients, imported collected later. superset ip_seqs definition. seq2pt named vector mapping sequence IDs patient IDs. dates vector isolate dates named sequence IDs. tree phylogenetic tree object class phylo constructed DNA alignment. can constructed using get_phylo_tree can tree object constructed isolates.

Threshold-free genomic cluster detection track transmission pathways health-care settings: genomic epidemiology analysis. Lancet Microbe, 3(9), e652–e662. doi:10.1016/S2666-5247(22)00115-X

title: Analyze Intra-Cluster Genetic Variation — intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis
text: function analyzes genetic diversity within clusters identifying variant sites calculating mutational spectra (e.g. transition transversion rates). uses DNA alignment vector cluster assignments (named sequence IDs) returns data frame summarizing diversity measures cluster well overall population.

title: Analyze Intra-Cluster Genetic Variation — intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis
code: intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis(clusters, dna_aln, var_pos)

title: Analyze Intra-Cluster Genetic Variation — intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis
text: clusters named vector cluster assignments (cluster IDs) names corresponding sequence IDs. dna_aln matrix representing DNA sequence alignment, rows sequences (named sequence IDs) columns represent nucleotide positions. var_pos logical vector indicating positions alignment variable.

title: Analyze Intra-Cluster Genetic Variation — intra_cluster_genetic_var_analysis
text: data frame one row per cluster (plus row overall population) containing number variable sites rates six mutation types. Mutation rates rounded three decimals.
